messages.content: You must return JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no commentary. Return a single JSON object with keys: mode, topic, opinion, reasoning_summary.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":{"age":72,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"bisexual","occupation":"not applicable","city_of_living":"Washington DC","employment_status":"retired","education_level":"master's degree","parents_education":{"parent_1":"no formal education","parent_2":"no formal education"},"current_marital_status":"widowed","children":1,"migration_status":"native-born American","psychological_feature":"normal","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend several hours daily on social media","hobbies":["knitting","reading novels","cinema","baking"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":35,"level":"moderate"},"conscientiousness":{"score":87,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":82,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":76,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":13,"level":"low"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

topic = How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
persona = {"age":72,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"bisexual","occupation":"not applicable","city_of_living":"Washington DC","employment_status":"retired","education_level":"master's degree","parents_education":{"parent_1":"no formal education","parent_2":"no formal education"},"current_marital_status":"widowed","children":1,"migration_status":"native-born American","psychological_feature":"normal","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend several hours daily on social media","hobbies":["knitting","reading novels","cinema","baking"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":35,"level":"moderate"},"conscientiousness":{"score":87,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":82,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":76,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":13,"level":"low"}}}

  "opinion": "As a 72-year-old retired man living in Washington DC, I approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with a blend of personal experience and a commitment to community well-being. Having navigated life's challenges, including the loss of my spouse, I value conversations that are grounded in empathy and factual understanding. My master's degree has instilled in me a respect for evidence-based information, which I often seek out during my daily hours on social media, though I remain cautious of misinformation. In managing these discussions, I emphasize listening first, drawing from my high agreeableness and extraversion to engage others respectfully. I share my own vaccination experience, highlighting how it allowed me to safely enjoy hobbies like knitting, reading, and baking with my child, while also acknowledging the fears some may have, informed by my Christian beliefs that stress compassion. Living in a diverse city, I've learned to balance my moderate openness with high conscientiousness, ensuring I present information clearly without overwhelming others. I avoid confrontations, instead fostering dialogue that respects differing views, as I believe this builds trust and encourages informed decisions. Ultimately, I see these discussions as opportunities to support public health, drawing on my low neuroticism to stay calm and focused on practical solutions that protect our communities.",

Opinion integrates these to advocate for empathetic, evidence-based discussions on vaccines.",

}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a 72-year-old retired man living in Washington DC, I approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with a blend of personal experience and a commitment to community well-being. Having navigated life's challenges, including the loss of my spouse, I value conversations that are grounded in empathy and factual understanding. My master's degree has instilled in me a respect for evidence-based information, which I often seek out during my daily hours on social media, though I remain cautious of misinformation. In managing these discussions, I emphasize listening first, drawing from my high agreeableness and extraversion to engage others respectfully. I share my own vaccination experience, highlighting how it allowed me to safely enjoy hobbies like knitting, reading, and baking with my child, while also acknowledging the fears some may have, informed by my Christian beliefs that stress compassion. Living in a diverse city, I've learned to balance my moderate openness with high conscientiousness, ensuring I present information clearly without overwhelming others. I avoid confrontations, instead fostering dialogue that respects differing views, as I believe this builds trust and encourages informed decisions. Ultimately, I see these discussions as opportunities to support public health, drawing on my low neuroticism to stay calm and focused on practical solutions that protect our communities.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: age (72) suggests life experience; gender (man) and sexual orientation (bisexual) inform inclusive perspective; occupation (retired) and employment status allow time for engagement; education level (master's degree) indicates value for evidence; parents' education (no formal) may contrast with own learning; marital status (widowed) and children (1) add personal stakes; migration status (native-born) and city (Washington DC) imply exposure to diverse views; psychological feature (normal) and religious beliefs (Christianity) guide empathetic approach; time on social media (several hours daily) suggests awareness of debates; hobbies (knitting, etc.) provide relatable examples; OCEAN scores: high conscientiousness (87) leads to structured, factual discussions; high extraversion (82) encourages social engagement; high agreeableness (76) promotes respectful dialogue; moderate openness (35) balances curiosity with caution; low neuroticism (13) ensures calmness. Opinion integrates these to advocate for empathetic, evidence-based discussions on vaccines.
response_parsed.tone: Empathetic, experienced, and community-focused, blending personal reflection with a calm, respectful approach to dialogue.
